text.title: client-conventions-reverse-engineering Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the client-conventions-reverse-engineering methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author skips lint config entirely and infers everything from PR diffs; guardrail contradicts the explicit config.
antipattern.root-cause: Author lazy with the explicit ground truth.
antipattern.fix: Always read lint/formatter configs first; cite paths.

antipattern.symptom: Inferred naming convention from 4 PRs (1 author); contradicts other authors' style.
antipattern.fix: Expand sample to >= 20 PRs across >= 5 distinct authors.

antipattern.symptom: Conventions split across 3 docs; agent reads one, misses the others.
antipattern.fix: Merge into a single repo-rules.md / AGENTS.md.

antipattern.symptom: 'Functions should be short' with no PR-comment or lint rule citation.
antipattern.fix: Either cite a source or drop the rule.
